name: Spicy Chicken Broccoli Rice Bowl
description: The default comfort-health meal. Real food, not diet food.
whyCookThis: Rotate through this when you want something reliable that still tastes alive. Protein, veg, carb, sauce and acid in one bowl — fast enough to make while tired.

steps.title: Cook the rice
steps.instruction: Rinse the rice until the water runs clearer. Add to a pot with water per the package and a pinch of salt. Cook covered, then rest 5-10 minutes off the heat.
tips: Salt the water — it seasons the rice from the inside.

steps.title: Mix the sauce
steps.instruction: Stir soy sauce, lime juice, chili and optional honey with a splash of water in a small bowl.
tips: The lime goes in the sauce now, but you'll add more fresh at the end.

steps.title: Cook the broccoli
steps.instruction: Steam for 3-5 minutes until bright green with some bite. Drain well.
tips: Don't overcook it; you want a little crunch left.

steps.title: Brown the chicken
steps.instruction: Salt the chicken. Heat a pan on medium-high, add olive oil, then the chicken in a single layer. Let it brown before stirring, and cook through.
tips: Salt before cooking, not after.
Don't crowd the pan — use a big pan or two batches so it browns instead of steaming.

steps.title: Add the aromatics
steps.instruction: Lower the heat slightly. Add garlic and ginger and stir for 30-60 seconds until fragrant.
tips: Add garlic late so it doesn't burn and turn bitter.

steps.title: Combine and serve
steps.instruction: Add broccoli and sauce, toss 1-2 minutes. Serve over rice, finish with sesame seeds and a fresh squeeze of lime.
tips: A final squeeze of lime makes the whole bowl taste fresher.

tasteFixes.fix: Add salt or a splash more soy sauce.
Add chili, or a tiny bit of honey.

easyUpgrades: Use chicken thigh for juicier, more forgiving meat.
Add frozen edamame for extra protein.
batchNotes: Good for 2 days. Rice is best cooked fresh, or cooled quickly and handled carefully.
safetyNotes: Cook chicken until safely done — a thermometer is the easiest way.
Cool rice quickly, refrigerate it, and avoid repeatedly reheating it.
Eat leftovers within 3-4 days, reheated until hot all the way through.

name: Super Veggie Lentil Bowl
description: The longevity-nerd bowl, adapted so it doesn't taste like punishment.
whyCookThis: Whole, warm, salty, acidic and a little spicy — packed with veg and lentils but genuinely good to eat. Keeps for days.

steps.title: Cook the lentils
steps.instruction: Rinse the lentils. Simmer gently in plenty of salted water until tender but not mushy, about 18-25 minutes. Drain.
tips: Salt the water — it seasons the lentils as they cook.

steps.title: Brown the mushrooms
steps.instruction: Put mushrooms in a pan with a splash of water. Cover 2-3 minutes to release moisture, then uncover and cook off the water. Add olive oil and keep cooking until browned.
tips: The browning is where a lot of the flavour comes from — don't rush it.

steps.title: Bloom the spices
steps.instruction: Add garlic, ginger, cumin, smoked paprika, chili, salt and pepper to the mushrooms. Stir 30-60 seconds until fragrant.
tips: Toasting spices in the oil makes them taste deeper, not dusty.

steps.title: Cook the vegetables
steps.instruction: Steam the broccoli and cauliflower for 4-6 minutes until tender.

steps.title: Combine and finish
steps.instruction: Mix lentils, mushrooms and veg. Add the vinegar. Serve with olive oil, walnuts, and rice if you want it more filling.
tips: Don't skip the vinegar — it's what makes it taste alive.

tasteFixes.fix: Add more olive oil, salt and smoked paprika.
Stir a spoon of Greek yogurt in after serving.

batchNotes: Keeps 3-4 days stored properly. Store rice separately if using.
safetyNotes: Cool leftovers quickly in shallow containers.
Eat within 3-4 days and reheat until hot all the way through.

name: Mushroom-Walnut Tomato Pasta
description: Healthy pasta that still feels like pasta.
whyCookThis: Mushrooms and walnuts make the sauce savoury and filling, so a whole-grain bowl of pasta actually satisfies. Great for leftovers.

steps.title: Start the sauce base
steps.instruction: Heat olive oil in a pan. Add onion and carrot with a pinch of salt and cook 5-8 minutes until softened.

steps.title: Brown the mushrooms
steps.instruction: Add mushrooms. Cook until they release their water and it mostly evaporates, then keep going until they darken and smell savoury.
tips: This browning step is most of the flavour — be patient.

steps.title: Add garlic and tomato paste
steps.instruction: Add garlic and tomato paste and stir for 1-2 minutes until the paste darkens slightly.
tips: Frying the tomato paste briefly makes it taste deeper and less raw.

steps.title: Simmer the sauce
steps.instruction: Add passata, soy sauce, balsamic, Italian herbs, walnuts, pepper and a little salt. Simmer 10-15 minutes.
tips: Soy sauce isn't there to make it Asian — it adds savoury depth.
Balsamic balances the tomato.

steps.title: Cook the pasta
steps.instruction: Boil the pasta in well-salted water. Save a mug of pasta water before draining.
tips: Salt the pasta water generously.

steps.title: Combine and finish
steps.instruction: Add pasta to the sauce with a splash of pasta water and stir until glossy. Stir in spinach at the end, and serve with parmesan if you like.
tips: Pasta water loosens and binds the sauce so it clings to the pasta.

tasteFixes.fix: Simmer it longer, or add a little olive oil.

easyUpgrades: Use chickpea pasta for more protein.
Add a spoon of Greek yogurt or ricotta after serving.
batchNotes: Excellent for leftovers — the sauce freezes well. Pasta is best fresh, but leftovers are still fine.
safetyNotes: Cool leftovers quickly in shallow containers.
Eat within 3-4 days and reheat until hot all the way through.

name: Sweet Potato Chickpea Curry
description: The forgiving one-pot. Warm, cozy, and hard to fully ruin.
whyCookThis: When you want something comforting with almost no technique. One pot, mostly pantry, and very hard to mess up.

steps.title: Cook the base
steps.instruction: Heat olive oil in a pot. Add onion and a pinch of salt and cook 5-8 minutes until soft.

steps.title: Add aromatics
steps.instruction: Add garlic and ginger and stir for 30-60 seconds.

steps.title: Bloom the spices
steps.instruction: Add curry powder, cumin, turmeric and chili. Stir 30 seconds. If the pot looks dry, add a tiny splash of water.
tips: Frying the spices briefly before the liquid goes in deepens the flavour.

steps.title: Simmer
steps.instruction: Add sweet potato, chickpeas, coconut milk, and enough stock to almost cover. Simmer 20-25 minutes until the sweet potato is soft.
tips: Stir occasionally so nothing catches on the bottom.

steps.title: Add greens and finish
steps.instruction: Stir in spinach until wilted and tender. Add lime juice, then taste and adjust the salt.
tips: Salt matters a lot here — sweet potato tastes flat without enough of it.
Add the lime at the end.

tasteFixes.fix: Add a spoon of yogurt after serving.

easyUpgrades: Add peanut butter for a richer curry.
batchNotes: Good for 3-4 days and freezes well. Store rice separately.
safetyNotes: Cool leftovers quickly in shallow containers.
Eat within 3-4 days and reheat until hot all the way through.

name: Nutty Berry Protein Bowl
description: The no-cook backup for mornings, evenings, or low-energy days.
whyCookThis: For days when cooking is not happening. Three minutes, no heat, genuinely filling and high in protein.

steps.title: Add the yogurt
steps.instruction: Spoon the yogurt into a bowl.

steps.title: Add the berries
steps.instruction: Scatter the frozen berries on top; they'll thaw into the yogurt.
tips: Use enough berries — they carry the flavour.

steps.title: Top and mix
steps.instruction: Add nuts, chia seeds, cacao, and any optional oats and protein powder. Mix, and let it sit 5 minutes if you want the chia and oats to soften.
tips: Cinnamon or cacao makes it taste like more of a treat.

tasteFixes.fix: Add a small amount of honey.
Add protein powder, or use Greek yogurt.

easyUpgrades: Add a scoop of protein powder.
Stir in oats the night before for an overnight version.
Add a spoon of nut butter.
batchNotes: Best fresh, but you can pre-mix dry toppings into small jars for the week.
safetyNotes: Keep yogurt refrigerated; don't leave the bowl out more than 2 hours.
